_description: LLM provider defaults. popular_models drives the ordered global selector and offline fallback independently of token constraints. POLICY (2026-07-23): popular_models lists only models with >= 1M-token context windows — providers whose models are all below 1M carry an empty list and contribute nothing to the global selector (their models stay reachable via the live per-provider fetch_models dropdown). Connection config drives native SDK and LangChain paths; model specs are enriched by model_registry.json (OpenRouter cache).

agent._description: Agent execution defaults. recursion_limit and compaction.ratio are sourced from the environment (AGENT_RECURSION_LIMIT, COMPACTION_RATIO via core.config.Settings) — these JSON values are last-resort fallbacks when the env vars aren't loaded (e.g. one-off CLI scripts that bypass Settings). default_temperature stays JSON-resident since it has no env override yet.

openai._models_note: GPT-5.6 family (sol/terra/luna) GA 2026-07-09. gpt-5.5 / gpt-5.4 remain GA as legacy tiers. Dropped: gpt-5.2 (new requests blocked 2026-06-30), o3 (API shutdown 2026-12-11), o4-mini (API shutdown 2026-10-23) — they fall back to _default constraints if still selected before shutdown.

gemini._detection_patterns_note: 'gemma' makes is_model_valid_for_provider accept the API-served Gemma models (gemma-4-*-it) on gemini nodes — without it the four agent-path call sites silently swap gemma to default_model. Cerebras keeps its own 'gemma-4' pattern for its gemma-4-31b (no -it suffix); is_model_valid_for_provider is per-provider so both coexist. Only the runtime-unused detect_provider_from_model classifier prefers gemini for gemma-* names (dict order).
gemini._models_note: Real (concrete) model names only — no -latest rolling aliases. The curated list (max_output_tokens keys) serves BOTH the Gemini Developer API (AIza keys) and Vertex / Agent Platform (AQ. keys); gemini-* entries live-verified on both endpoints (2026-06-12). Vertex 404s on -latest aliases and its models.list rejects API keys, which is why the dropdown is curated instead of live-fetched. Gemma exception: gemma-4-31b-it + gemma-4-26b-a4b-it are served ONLY on the Developer API (free tier, ai.google.dev/gemma/docs/core/gemma_on_gemini_api, verified 2026-07-11) — Vertex keys must not see them, hence vertex_incompatible_models below. Gemma 4 supports systemInstruction and function calling natively (unlike the gemma-3 era); max output is undocumented, so the curated 8192 is a conservative fallback (registry cache wins when present).
gemini._vertex_incompatible_models_note: Curated models that only exist on the Gemini Developer API, filtered out of fetch_models when the key is a Vertex / Agent Platform key (GeminiProvider._vertex). Gemma is Model Garden self-deploy on Vertex (the 26B MaaS variant needs a different '-maas' id on a different surface), so the plain ids 404 there.
gemini._incompatible_models_note: Models that the dropdown should hide because they need a different API endpoint than `generate_content`. antigravity-preview-05-2026 requires the Interactions API; selecting it currently surfaces a `google.genai.errors.APIError`. ChatUnifier reads this list in fetch_models and filters before returning. Drop entries here when the unifier gains routing support for the alternate endpoint.

groq._popular_note: Empty per the 1M-context policy — no Groq-served model reaches 1M (all top out at 131072). Models remain reachable via live fetch_models.
groq._models_note: Default moved to openai/gpt-oss-120b (production route). Groq announced deprecation of llama-3.3-70b-versatile and llama-3.1-8b-instant on 2026-06-17 (console.groq.com/docs/deprecations); kept in the list while still served — they fall back to _default constraints after removal.

cerebras._popular_note: Empty per the 1M-context policy — no Cerebras model reaches 1M (all 131072). Models remain reachable via live fetch_models.
cerebras._models_note: Per inference-docs.cerebras.ai 2026-07-11: gpt-oss-120b is the only production model (paid tier 131k context / 40k max completion); zai-glm-4.7 and gemma-4-31b are preview. qwen-3-235b* variants are shut down (migration target: gpt-oss-120b).

xai._popular_note: grok-4.5 (flagship, released 2026-07-08) and grok-3 excluded per the 1M-context policy (500K / 131072); reachable via live fetch_models. Default stays grok-4.20 (2M) so the default is selectable from the global list.
xai._models_note: grok-4.5 (released 2026-07-08): 500K context, configurable reasoning effort (default high), $2/$6 per MTok. Output cap undocumented by xAI — curated 131072 matches the rest of the grok family; registry cache wins when present.

deepseek._popular_note: deepseek-chat / deepseek-reasoner dropped from the global list per the 1M-context policy (131072); reachable via live fetch_models.

kimi._popular_note: Only kimi-k3 meets the 1M-context policy (k2.x are 262144); k2.x remain reachable via live fetch_models.
kimi._models_note: kimi-k3 GA (platform.kimi.ai): 2.8T params, 1M context, vision + tool calling + structured outputs, $3/$15 per MTok ($0.30 cache-hit). Default max_completion_tokens 131072 (configurable up to the full window). k2.x kept as cheaper tiers.

mistral._popular_note: Empty per the 1M-context policy — Mistral tops out at 262144 (large/medium). Models remain reachable via live fetch_models.

ollama._description: Local Ollama server (OpenAI-compatible /v1). No `default_model` because the dropdown should reflect the user's actually-pulled models — fetch_models hits the user's saved {provider}_proxy URL and returns whatever Ollama reports; when the server is offline we want an empty list (a 'start your server' cue), not a placeholder name the user almost certainly doesn't have.

lmstudio._description: Local LM Studio server (pure OpenAI-compatible). Same no-default-model rule as ollama — the dropdown reflects what the user has loaded in LM Studio, not a fake placeholder.
